din84.source: DIN 84 / ISO 1207 slotted cheese head machine screw, M10 x 30 (representative length; M10 is the largest size common to every standard in this family — M12 exceeds the ISO 1207 / DIN 84 range). dk=16.0 and k=6.0 tabulated, cross-checked against the Aramfix DIN 84 datasheet and a generic DIN slotted-head dimension chart. Slot width n=drive_w=2.5 and slot depth t=drive_t=2.4 tabulated (generic DIN slotted-head chart). crown_r=0.5 is a representative top-edge break (not tabulated). d_shank=10.0 (M10 nominal major); shank drawn as a smooth envelope cylinder (no thread).

iso1207.source: ISO 1207 slotted cheese head, M10 — same envelope as DIN 84 (dk=16.0, k=6.0, slot 2.5/2.4), aliases the DIN base. Confirmed vs the Aramfix DIN 84 datasheet + generic DIN slotted-head chart.

din85.source: DIN 85 / ISO 1580 slotted pan head machine screw, M10 x 30 (representative length; M10, not M12 — M12 exceeds the DIN 85 / ISO 1580 range). dk=20.0 and k=6.0 tabulated, cross-checked across four public tables (fasteners.eu DIN 85, Vidasan DIN 85/ISO 1580, Torqbolt ISO 1580, Fuller Fasteners DIN 85 — all give dk max 20.0, k max 6.0 for M10; the 16 mm dk is the M8 row). Slot width n=drive_w=2.5 and slot depth t=drive_t=2.6 tabulated (Ferrobolt DIN 85, t range 2.56-2.81). The pan head is drawn as a shallow spherical-cap dome of height k (a representative envelope of the tabulated dk/k). d_shank=10.0 (M10 major).

iso1580.source: ISO 1580 slotted pan head, M10 — same envelope as DIN 85 (dk=20.0, k=6.0), aliases the DIN base. Confirmed vs Vidasan DIN 85/ISO 1580 + Torqbolt ISO 1580 tables.

din963.source: DIN 963 / ISO 2009 slotted countersunk (flat) head machine screw, M10 x 30 (representative length; M10, not M12). dk=18.0 (max top diameter) and k=5.0 (head height) tabulated, cross-checked against the Westfield Fasteners DIN 963 table and the Ratnamani DIN 963 table. Slot width n=drive_w=2.5 (tabulated range 2.0-2.6) and slot depth t=drive_t=2.3 (tabulated range 2.0-2.6, mid). The head is drawn as a straight cone frustum from the M10 major (d_shank=10.0) at the bearing plane to the tabulated dk=18.0 over the tabulated k=5.0; the true countersink is 90 degrees, so the envelope cone angle is representative (it preserves the tabulated dk and k rather than the exact angle). d_shank=10.0 (M10 major).

iso2009.source: ISO 2009 slotted countersunk head, M10 — same screw as DIN 963, aliases the DIN base. ISO 2009 tabulates dk max 18.3 vs DIN 963 18.0 (a DIN-vs-ISO table difference of 0.3 mm, negligible at envelope scale), k=5.0. Confirmed vs Lubker ISO 2009 + Westfield DIN 963 tables.

din966.source: DIN 966 / ISO 2010 slotted raised countersunk (oval) head machine screw, M10 x 30 (representative length; M10, not M12). dk=18.0 and countersunk head height k=5.0 tabulated (Vidasan DIN 966/ISO 7047 table for dk; Westfield / Ratnamani DIN 963 tables for k, shared with DIN 963 at M10). Raised-lens height raised_f=2.5 tabulated nominal (Vidasan DIN 966, f nominal ~2.5, max 5.0). Slot width n=drive_w=2.5 and depth t=drive_t=2.3 as DIN 963 M10 (tabulated ranges 2.0-2.6). The cone is the DIN 963 frustum (representative angle) with a spherical-cap lens of height raised_f on top. d_shank=10.0 (M10 major).

iso2010.source: ISO 2010 slotted raised countersunk head, M10 — same screw as DIN 966 (dk~18, k=5.0, raised lens), aliases the DIN base. Confirmed vs Vidasan DIN 966/ISO 7047 + DIN 963 M10 tables.

iso7045.source: ISO 7045 / DIN 7985 cross-recess (Phillips/Pozidriv) pan head machine screw, M10 x 30 (representative length; M10, not M12). dk=20.0 and k=7.5 tabulated, cross-checked across Vipa (ISO 7045/DIN 7985), Wanhong (ISO 7045 pan cross) and Fuller Fasteners ISO 7045 tables (all dk max 20.0, k max 7.5 for M10). Cross recess is ISO 4757 recess No. 4 for M10: drive_m=10.1 is the tabulated surface reference diameter (type H, ISO 4757/ISO 7045) and drive_t=5.5 the recess penetration (ISO 7721 range ~5.2-5.8, mid). drive_w=2.8 is a representative recess arm width (ISO 4757 does not tabulate a simple arm width). The cross recess is a representative tapered-pyramidal icon, not the dimensioned ISO 4757 curve. d_shank=10.0 (M10 major).

din7985.source: DIN 7985 cross-recess pan head, M10 — same envelope as ISO 7045 (dk=20.0, k=7.5, cross recess No. 4), aliases the ISO base. Confirmed vs Vipa ISO 7045/DIN 7985 + Fuller ISO 7045 tables.

iso7046.source: ISO 7046 / DIN 965 cross-recess countersunk (flat) head machine screw, M10 x 30 (representative length; M10, not M12). dk=18.3 (max) and k=5.0 (max) tabulated, cross-checked across the BSN Fastener ISO 7046-1 catalog, the Lubker ISO 7046-1 table and the Fasten.it ISO 7046-1 table (all dk max 18.3, k max 5.0 for M10; ISO 2009 slotted csk matches). Cross recess is ISO 4757 recess No. 4: drive_m=10.1 tabulated surface reference diameter (type H). drive_t=4.0 is a representative recess depth chosen < head height k=5.0 (the true ISO 7721 penetration ~5.2 mm would pierce the thin envelope head floor); drive_w=2.8 representative arm width. The cross recess is a representative tapered-pyramidal icon, not the dimensioned ISO 4757 curve. d_shank=10.0 (M10 major).

din965.source: DIN 965 cross-recess countersunk head, M10 — same envelope as ISO 7046 (dk=18.3, k=5.0, cross recess No. 4), aliases the ISO base. Confirmed vs BSN + Lubker ISO 7046-1 tables.

iso7047.source: ISO 7047 cross-recess raised countersunk (oval) head machine screw, M10 x 30 (representative length; M10, not M12). Ships with NO DIN alias: the historical DIN cross raised-countersunk (DIN 966 form H) was withdrawn/merged and din966 is the slotted raised base here, so there is no clean current 1:1 DIN number (a deliberate skip, like DIN 562 / DIN 80701). dk=18.3 and countersunk head height k=5.0 tabulated as the ISO common head style shared with ISO 7046 / ISO 2009 (cross-checked BSN + Lubker + Fasten.it ISO 7046-1 / ISO 2009 M10 rows). Raised-lens height raised_f=2.5 (DIN 966 / ISO 7047 equivalence, Vidasan). Cross recess ISO 4757 No. 4: drive_m=10.1 surface reference diameter; drive_t=4.0 representative depth < k=5.0; drive_w=2.8 representative arm width. The cross recess is a representative tapered-pyramidal icon, not the ISO 4757 curve. d_shank=10.0 (M10 major).
